body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: uurecall literal search fix
body.intention: I intend that we verify the uurecall fix has no silent regressions in semantic recall quality or tool latency.
body.description:
**What changed:** uurecall now filters tickets by all statuses, suppresses semantic results below 0.4 score threshold, and adds palace nodes as a fifth search source.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Semantic threshold of 0.4 may over-suppress legitimate but weakly-scored matches for rare or domain-specific terms
- All-status ticket loading may return too many closed/design tickets, making Tickets section noisy for common terms
- Palace search adds a DB round-trip; if palace is slow or offline, uurecall latency increases noticeably
- Template/specialization question (Q1 in ticket) may resurface: if single-tool approach still misses cases, pressure builds toward splitting

**Signals to watch:**
- `uurecall T-inference-proxy-mini-rack` returns ticket in Tickets section (the regression test from the ticket)
- No increase in uurecall wall-clock time > 500ms vs baseline
- Semantic section not empty for conceptual queries (e.g. `uurecall spreading activation`) — threshold not too aggressive

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-22 after T-uurecall-search-gaps closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
